news-korea-econ.description: Bangladesh expects its first nuclear power plant to supply up to 15% of national electricity, strengthening energy security and reducing dependence on imported fossil fuels

news-global-econ.title: Vintage photos reveal what grocery shopping looked like in the 1960s
news-global-econ.description: Vintage 1960s grocery ads show sirloin steak at 78 cents per pound, but adjusting for inflation reveals surprising truths about today's prices.

news-global-econ.title: Katherine Heigl lists her Utah home for $10.6M as family prepares to downsize
news-global-econ.description: Katherine Heigl listed her 8,352-square-foot Utah Mountains home for $10.6 million as she and husband Josh Kelley look to downsize their estate.

news-global-econ.title: New Germany sex-crime figures reignite migration fight as exploitation probe expands
news-global-econ.description: Germany recorded 751 group rape cases in 2025 with 578 non-German suspects identified, as a Nuremberg grooming gang investigation continues to expand.

news-global-econ.description: Jannik Sinner enters the Wimbledon final against Alexander Zverev on a dominant streak, having lost just one set the entire grass court tournament.

news-global-econ.title: Netanyahu details 'final' conversation with Lindsey Graham, says senator challenged his push to end Israel aid
news-global-econ.description: Benjamin Netanyahu reveals Lindsey Graham refused to let Israel phase out U.S. military aid during what became one of their final conversations.

news-global-soc.title: Trump Administration Officials Remember Lindsey Graham: 'Devoted Public Servant'
news-global-soc.description:
Several officials within the Trump administration remembered Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC) after his office shared that he died at the age of 71 from a "brief and sudden illness," describing Graham as a "devoted public servant" and "great American and Patriot."

news-global-soc.description: U.S. oil production hit a record 21.1 million barrels per day in 2025 as fossil fuels still supply 86% of global energy, a new report reveals.

news-global-soc.title: Another US citizen tests positive for Ebola virus while working in Congo amid record outbreak
news-global-soc.description: A second U.S. citizen tested positive for Ebola while working for a humanitarian organization in the Democratic Republic of the Congo amid the outbreak.

news-global-soc.title: One Player Killed, Two Crowd Members Injured in Shooting at Harlem Basketball Tourney
news-global-soc.description:
One player was killed and two crowd members were injured during a shooting at the Kingdome Basketball Tournament in Harlem about 10:30 p.m. Friday.
